root: ^The great hall of Pandemonium falls silent. Every seat is taken. The lords of Hell sit in their appointed places, their faces lit by the naphtha lamps above.
^Satan rises from his throne. His voice carries to every corner of the vast chamber.
^We are met. The first council of our new dominion. You know why we are here — to decide our course. Shall we accept this exile, or reclaim what was taken? I would hear your counsel.
^Moloch is first to his feet, armour still scorched from the fall.
^My vote is open war! Nothing less! We have weapons. We have fury. Let us storm the gates of Heaven and tear down His throne by force!
^The hall rumbles with approval from the warriors among the host.
^What say we to them? We may not win — but we will make Him bleed for what He did to us!
^"Moloch speaks with fire. Let him continue."
^"Courage without strategy is suicide. Who else would speak?"

c-0: ^What is the worst that can befall us? We are already damned! Already cast out! He cannot kill us — we are immortal! So I say: let us make war eternal. Let Heaven know no peace!
^He slams his fist upon the table. The sound echoes like thunder.
c-1: ^Moloch snarls but yields the floor. The hall murmurs — some disappointed, some relieved.

belial_speaks: ^Belial rises — smooth, unhurried, every movement deliberate. Where Moloch burned, Belial gleams.
^War? Against the Almighty? Let us be honest with ourselves. We lost. Comprehensively. His thunder shattered our legions like glass.
^I say we make the best of what we have. This place — it is not so terrible. We are free from His laws. Free from His endless demands for worship. Is that not what we wanted?
^Let us build here. Live here. Make Hell our own paradise. In time, perhaps He will forget us — and we shall have peace.
^The hall is quieter now. Some nod thoughtfully.
^"There is wisdom in acceptance. But is it not also cowardice?"
^"Peace built on defeat is no peace at all. Who else?"

c-0: ^Belial's smile does not waver, but something hardens behind his eyes.
^Call it what you will, my Lord. I call it survival. The brave dead are still dead.
c-1: ^Belial bows and sits. He does not look offended — if anything, he looks amused.
c-2: ^We have eternity, my Lord. There is no shame in patience. Let us use this exile to become something new — something He never intended.

mammon_speaks: ^Mammon stands, his fingers stained with the gold dust of Hell's mines.
^Why do we speak of Heaven at all? Look around you! Look what we have built in mere days! Hell's riches are beyond counting.

^I say we turn inward. Mine deeper. Build greater. Let Heaven keep its cold perfection — we shall have wealth, splendour, and freedom. Why grovel for readmission to a court where we must kneel forever?
^Beelzebub has been silent through all of this. Now he stands.
^You have heard three paths: war, peace, and industry. But I propose a fourth.
^There is a rumour — a prophecy — of a new creation. A world called Earth, and upon it, creatures called Man, beloved of the Almighty.
^What if we could corrupt this new creation? Strike not at Heaven directly, but at what He loves most?
^One volunteer. One of us to make the journey through Chaos, find this new world, and turn His precious creatures against Him. Revenge without war. Victory without battle.
^"This is the way. Cunning over force."
^"It is a coward's path — but perhaps a wise one."
^"Who would dare such a journey alone?"

c-0: ^Beelzebub nods. The hall erupts in debate — voices rising, falling, clashing.
c-1: ^Moloch spits in disgust. Belial raises an eyebrow. Mammon looks calculating.
^Call it what you will — but who among us has the courage to undertake it?
c-2: ^Silence. The lords look at one another. The journey through Chaos is no small thing — it is the void between worlds, formless and lethal.

volunteer: ^The hall falls silent. No one moves. The journey through Chaos — past the gates of Hell, through the formless void — it could mean oblivion even for an immortal.
^Moloch, who called for war, says nothing. Belial studies his hands. Mammon looks away.
^"The task is mine. I led you into war — I shall lead you to victory."
^"I would not ask of any what I would not do myself."
^"None of you would survive the journey. I alone have the strength."

c-0: ^The fallen angels rise as one. The sound of their acclamation shakes the pillars of Pandemonium.
^In this moment, you are more than a fallen angel. You are their sovereign, their champion, their last hope.
c-1: ^A murmur of something almost like shame passes through the lords. Then admiration. Then thunderous approval.
^You honour us, my Lord. We shall hold Pandemonium in your absence.
c-2: ^Pride. Defiance. The hall recognizes both. Moloch slams his fist in salute. Even Belial nods with something approaching respect.
^Then go with our strength behind you. All of Hell shall await your return.

conclusion: ^The council is ended. The decision is made.
^As the fallen angels file out of the great hall, Satan remains. He looks up at the vaulted ceiling of Pandemonium — this palace they built from nothing, in the pit of damnation.
^Beyond the gates of Hell lies Chaos. Beyond Chaos lies the new world. Beyond the new world lies revenge.
^Satan turns toward the great doors. The journey begins.
